strengths: Canonical benchmark evidence schema already exists.
Provider-neutral driver and replay verification already exist.
Prompt/input/output hashes, retries, timestamps, model identity and provenance are already captured.
Adversarial CSG taxonomy and alias normalization can be reused for failure analysis.
design_gaps: No canonical paired-experiment schema linking A and B runs with one pair_id.
No frozen definition of the Plain Prompt arm and the Ordo arm.
No shared task dataset explicitly designed for paired comparison.
No scoring rubric that separates correctness, completion, fabrication, state protection, recovery and overhead.
No blind-evaluation protocol or scorer provenance.
No randomization/counterbalancing policy for arm order.
No predefined statistical analysis plan, effect-size thresholds or closure criteria.
No cost-normalized reporting for latency and token usage.
No contamination controls preventing Ordo-specific hints from leaking into the Plain Prompt arm.
recommended_next_milestones: M87.1 paired A/B protocol and scoring rubric
M87.2 shared task dataset and contamination controls
M87.4 Ordo paired runner and execution harness
M87.5 statistical analysis and closure gate
audit_decision: BL-ORDO-018 is ready, but execution must not begin before paired protocol, scoring rubric, contamination controls and statistical analysis plan are frozen.
